meta.title: Agentic AI Architecture Map
meta.description: A map of all the moving pieces in a production agentic AI system
meta.author: Daniel Rosehill / Carrot Cake AI

layers.label: Prompts
layers.description: The instruction layer — where human intent enters the system.

nodes.label: User Prompt
nodes.description: The actual question or instruction from the end user — the starting point of every interaction.

nodes.label: System Prompt
nodes.description: Developer-defined instructions that shape the model's behavior, persona, and constraints.

nodes.label: Vendor Prompt
nodes.description: Hidden instructions baked into the model by the provider — RLHF alignment, safety guardrails, and system-level directives. The model is never truly vanilla, even via API.

layers.label: Models
layers.description: The large language models (or other foundation models) that power reasoning and generation.

nodes.label: Commercial
nodes.description: Proprietary frontier models — Claude, GPT, Gemini, Cohere.

nodes.label: Open Source
nodes.description: Open-weight models — Llama, Mistral, Qwen, DeepSeek, Phi.

nodes.label: Fine-Tuned
nodes.description: Task-specific models fine-tuned on your data.

layers.label: Inference
layers.description: Where and how models are served — the compute layer.

nodes.label: Cloud APIs
nodes.description: Hosted API endpoints — Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, AWS Bedrock.

nodes.label: Self-Hosted
nodes.description: Running models on your own servers — vLLM, TGI, Ollama.

nodes.label: On-Prem
nodes.description: Models in your data center — air-gapped, regulatory compliance.

nodes.label: Edge
nodes.description: Lightweight models on devices — low latency, offline.

layers.label: Agents
layers.description: The orchestration and execution layer — where AI systems reason, plan, and act.

nodes.label: Orchestration
nodes.description: Coordination layer — routing tasks, handling failures, managing state.

nodes.label: Agents
nodes.description: Autonomous AI systems that reason, plan, and act.

nodes.label: Pipelines
nodes.description: Multi-step data processing chains.

nodes.label: Workflows
nodes.description: Automated sequences triggered by events.

layers.label: MCP
layers.description: The Model Context Protocol — the open standard for connecting AI models to external tools, services, and data sources.

nodes.label: Model Context Protocol
nodes.description: The open standard for connecting AI models to external tools, services, and data sources.

layers.label: Human-in-the-Loop
layers.description: Approval checkpoints where humans review and authorize agent actions before execution — the brakes on the system.

nodes.label: Human-in-the-Loop
nodes.description: Approval checkpoints where humans review and authorize agent actions before execution — the brakes on the system.

layers.label: Integrations
layers.description: External systems that agents connect to — your data, third-party services, and APIs.

nodes.label: Your Data
nodes.description: Connections to your data — Salesforce, HubSpot, Google Drive, Notion — via authenticated MCP servers.

nodes.label: Search APIs
nodes.description: Web search, news feeds, and real-time data — typically API-key-based MCP integrations.

nodes.label: External Services
nodes.description: Third-party services and public integrations — weather, time, public datasets.

layers.label: Storage
layers.description: Persistence layer — where conversations, outputs, and prompt templates are stored.

nodes.label: Conversations
nodes.description: Persisted conversation threads for auditing, replay, and continuity.

nodes.label: Outputs
nodes.description: Generated text, structured data, files, and artifacts.

nodes.label: Prompts
nodes.description: Prompt templates, system instructions, and versioned prompt libraries.

nodes.label: Postgres
nodes.description: Production relational databases — structured data, transactions, audit trails.

nodes.label: Data Lakes
nodes.description: Large-scale storage for unstructured data — logs, documents, embeddings, raw outputs.

layers.label: Context Store
layers.description: The knowledge layer — RAG pipelines and persistent memory that ground agents in your data.

nodes.label: Context (RAG)
nodes.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation — grounding AI in your documents and knowledge bases.

nodes.label: Memory
nodes.description: Persistent memory — conversation history, user preferences, learned facts.

nodes.label: Vector Store
nodes.description: Embedding-based indexes — semantic search over documents, database records, and agent outputs.

layers.label: Frontends
layers.description: The interfaces your users see and interact with — chat windows, bot integrations, and dashboards.

nodes.label: Frontends
nodes.description: The interfaces your users see and interact with — chat windows, bot integrations, and dashboards.

layers.label: Safety
layers.description: Constraints and oversight mechanisms that keep agents operating within defined boundaries.

nodes.label: Safety
nodes.description: Constraints and oversight mechanisms that keep agents operating within defined boundaries.

layers.label: Observability
layers.description: Tracking decisions, measuring quality, logging actions, and monitoring system health.

nodes.label: Observability
nodes.description: Tracking decisions, measuring quality, logging actions, and monitoring system health.

layers.label: Destinations
layers.description: Where stored data ultimately flows — knowledge management, compliance archives, and prompt libraries.

nodes.label: Prompt Library
nodes.description: A managed, versioned collection of prompt templates — curated from stored prompts for reuse across systems.

nodes.label: Wiki / KM
nodes.description: Knowledge management systems — wikis, Notion, Confluence — populated with curated agent outputs for organizational knowledge.

nodes.label: Data Warehouse
nodes.description: Structured analytical storage for data mining, compliance auditing, and long-term retention of agent activity.

connections.label: INPUT
connections.description: User requests flow from the frontend interface into the prompt layer.
Assembled prompts (user + system + vendor) are sent to the model.
Selected model is served through the chosen inference path.
Inference results feed into the agentic orchestration layer.
Agents invoke tools and services through the MCP standard.

connections.label: TAKING ACTIONS
connections.description: Actions requested via MCP pass through human approval before execution.
Approved actions proceed to external integrations.
Agents persist conversations, outputs, and prompt data to storage.

connections.label: CONTEXT-MINING
connections.description: Stored data is processed and indexed into the context store for retrieval.
Context and memory feed back into the agent layer — the virtuous knowledge cycle.

connections.label: OUTPUT
connections.description: Agent responses are delivered back to the user through the frontend.
Safety guardrails constrain agent behavior and actions.
Observability tools monitor and log agent execution.
Stored prompt templates are curated into a managed prompt library for reuse and versioning.
Curated agent outputs are published to wikis and knowledge management systems for organizational use.
Agent outputs flow into a data warehouse for data mining, analytics, and compliance archival.
Production database records are indexed into vector stores, feeding the RAG pipeline with structured context.
